examples.title: BSOD Diagnosis from Stop Code
examples.description: Diagnose a recurring BSOD from stop code and recent system changes.

input.context: User reports IRQL_NOT_LESS_OR_EQUAL (0x0000000A) occurring 3 times in past week. Started after installing new USB Wi-Fi adapter. Windows 11 23H2, Build 22631.3737.

expected_output.diagnostics_table:
| Component | Status | Evidence | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| USB Wi-Fi adapter driver | Suspect [DRV] | Timing correlation with install | Verify driver version, check vendor site |
| Kernel IRQL | Elevated [DRV] | Stop code 0x0A indicates driver IRQL violation | Driver verifier on suspect device |
| System stability | Degraded [OBS] | 3 BSODs in 7 days | Immediate investigation required |
expected_output.remediation:
1. Run `verifier /standard /driver usbwifi.sys` to enable Driver Verifier [GEN]
2. Check Device Manager for driver version and date [OBS]
3. Download latest driver from vendor site [GEN]
4. If persists, remove adapter and test stability [DRV]

examples.title: Performance Optimization for Slow Boot
examples.description: Diagnose and optimize slow boot time on Windows 11 system.

input.context: Boot time increased from 15s to 90s+ over past month. SSD system drive (NVMe). 16GB RAM. i7-12700K. Multiple startup programs. Windows 11 23H2.

expected_output.performance_table:
| Metric | Current | Threshold | Status | Action |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Boot time | 90s+ [OBS] | <30s | Critical | Investigate startup items |
| Startup programs | Unknown | <10 | Warning | Run `Get-CimInstance Win32_StartupCommand` [GEN] |
| SSD health | Unknown | >90% | Check | Run `Get-PhysicalDisk | Get-StorageReliabilityCounter` [GEN] |
| Fast Startup | Unknown | Enabled | Check | Verify in Power Options [GEN] |
expected_output.powershell_diagnostic:
```powershell
# Boot time diagnostic script
Get-WinEvent -LogName 'Microsoft-Windows-Diagnostics-Performance/Operational' -MaxEvents 10 |
  Where-Object { $_.Id -eq 100 } |
  Select-Object TimeCreated, @{N='BootTime_ms';E={$_.Properties[1].Value}} |
  Format-Table -AutoSize
```

examples.title: Security Hardening Quick Check (Low Energy)
examples.description: Quick security posture check at low energy level.

input.context: Quick check on security basics. Windows 11 Home edition. Single user. No domain.

expected_output.quick_check_table:
| Check | Command | Expected | Claim |
|---|---|---|---|
| Windows Defender | `Get-MpComputerStatus` | RealTimeProtection: True | [GEN] |
| Firewall | `Get-NetFirewallProfile` | All profiles enabled | [GEN] |
| BitLocker | `Get-BitLockerVolume` | Protection: On | [GEN] |
| Windows Update | `Get-WindowsUpdate` | No critical pending | [GEN] |
expected_output.energy_note: Low energy mode: quick check table only. No deep audit. Escalate if any check fails.
